ops.title: Workflow tool `args` passed as a JSON object can reach the script as a JSON-encoded string — parse defensively at the top of every script that reads args
ops.body:
Passing `args` to the Workflow tool as a real JSON object does not guarantee the script receives an object: observed live (2026-07-05) that `args` arrived as a JSON-encoded *string*, so `args.judgePrompt` was `undefined` and the agent ran with the literal prompt "undefined".

**How to apply:** start any args-consuming workflow script with
`const input = typeof args === 'string' ? JSON.parse(args) : args` and validate the required fields (throw early on missing/short values) before any `agent()` call. Related: [[workflow-resume-survives-pool-relaunches]] — after fixing the script file, relaunch with `scriptPath` + `resumeFromRunId`.
